task_name: Implement Local HTTP Mock Builder Adapter Route
goal: Implement the local HTTP mock Builder Adapter route using Node built-ins, existing fixtures, and no live Builder or external network behavior.
claims: The local HTTP mock route binds to 127.0.0.1 and handles only POST /api/bluepilot/builder-adapter/mock-run.
The route reuses local adapter core logic and existing fixtures.
The route blocks non-POST requests, non-JSON requests, invalid JSON, scope violations, blocked operations, reject, and pending human review.
The implementation adds no live Builder, external network, package, docs, examples, app, API, or frontend changes.
assumptions: Node built-in http is sufficient for the local mock route.
Refactoring adapter logic into a local core module is required to avoid temporary request files and logic duplication.
Binding to 127.0.0.1 for test execution satisfies the local-only contract.
dod: Existing CLI fixture tests still pass.
HTTP route fixture tests pass for six fixture cases plus non-POST, non-JSON, and invalid JSON.
stop_conditions: Package dependency or package script becomes necessary
Live Builder endpoint or external network behavior becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-012 contracted a local HTTP mock route: bind locally, reuse existing fixtures, keep mock=true and decision_ready=false, and do not add live Builder/network behavior.
